metaTitle: Inwood Pest Control | Same-Day NYC Exterminators
metaDescription: Professional pest control in Inwood, NY. Serving homes near Isham Park & Seaman Ave. Experts in roaches, mice, bed bugs & rats. Same-day service. Call now!
introParagraph: The Best Pest Control NYC delivers fast, effective pest control to Inwood residents from Dyckman Street to the Henry Hudson Bridge. Serving pre-war walk-ups along Broadway, family homes near Isham Park, and co-ops overlooking Inwood Hill Park, our licensed technicians understand the unique pest challenges facing this northernmost Manhattan neighborhood. Whether you're battling mice in a Sherman Avenue apartment or roaches near the A train station, we offer same-day service with proven results.
whyThisAreaSection: Inwood's proximity to Inwood Hill Park and the Harlem River creates constant pest pressure from wildlife and moisture-loving insects. The neighborhood's aging pre-war buildings along Dyckman and Academy Streets feature original plumbing and foundation gaps that invite rodents. Heavy foot traffic from the A and 1 train stations at 207th Street spreads bed bugs and cockroaches. Commercial corridors on Broadway and nearby restaurants provide abundant food sources. The mix of natural green spaces and dense residential blocks makes Inwood particularly vulnerable to seasonal pest surges.
propertyTypesSection: Inwood's housing stock consists largely of five- and six-story pre-war walk-ups with shared walls, interconnected basements, and vintage pipe chases that rodents navigate easily. Many buildings along Seaman Avenue and Post Avenue feature outdated garbage chutes and compactor rooms where roaches thrive. Garden-level apartments near Fort Tryon Park face moisture intrusion and ant problems. Co-op buildings overlooking the Hudson require comprehensive rodent-proofing as aging masonry develops cracks. Single-family rowhouses on side streets deal with basement mice and attic wildlife from adjacent park areas.
commonPestsSection: Mice top the list in Inwood, entering through foundation cracks and following steam pipe openings between floors. German cockroaches infest kitchens in multi-family buildings, spreading via shared plumbing. Bed bugs appear frequently in apartments near transit hubs and densely populated blocks. Norway rats emerge from park edges and riverfront areas, burrowing near building foundations. Ants invade ground-floor units during spring and summer. Residents also report squirrels and raccoons entering attics in homes bordering Inwood Hill Park.

faqs.question: How much does pest control cost in Inwood?
faqs.answer: General pest control in Inwood ranges from $175–$300 per treatment. Cockroach extermination costs $350–$550, while mouse or rat removal runs $350–$600. Bed bug heat or chemical treatments range from $1,200–$3,500 depending on infestation severity and apartment size. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing for all Inwood properties.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pest emergencies in Inwood?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency pest control service throughout Inwood, from Dyckman Street to 218th Street. Emergency services range from $350–$1,000 depending on the pest type and severity. Our technicians are familiar with buildings along Broadway, Seaman Avenue, and Academy Street, allowing us to respond rapidly to urgent roach, mouse, or bed bug situations.

faqs.question: Are your treatments safe for children and pets in Inwood apartments?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-registered products approved for residential use, with pet- and child-safe options available for all Inwood homes and apartments. Our technicians apply targeted treatments in cracks, crevices, and voids rather than broadcast spraying. We provide specific re-entry times and safety instructions. Many families in Inwood buildings choose our low-toxicity integrated pest management approaches.

faqs.question: Why do I keep seeing mice in my Inwood apartment?
faqs.answer: Pre-war Inwood buildings have interconnected pipe chases, shared wall voids, and aging foundations that mice travel through freely. If one apartment has mice, neighboring units often do too. Effective control requires treating the entire building, sealing entry points around radiator pipes and baseboards, and comprehensive exclusion work. Rodent-proofing costs $500–$3,000 depending on building size.

faqs.question: How do bed bugs spread in Inwood walk-ups?
faqs.answer: Bed bugs move between apartments through electrical outlets, baseboards, and shared walls in Inwood's multi-family buildings. High tenant turnover and proximity to the A and 1 train stations increase exposure risk. Guests, secondhand furniture, and laundry rooms also facilitate spread. Building-wide inspections help prevent re-infestation. Treatment costs $1,200–$3,500 depending on apartment size and infestation level.

faqs.question: Do you service buildings near Inwood Hill Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, we regularly service homes and apartments along Seaman Avenue, Indian Road, and surrounding streets near Inwood Hill Park. Properties near the park face unique challenges including wildlife intrusion, moisture-related pests, and seasonal ant activity. We provide wildlife removal ($900–$4,000) for squirrels and raccoons, plus perimeter treatments to prevent park-based pests from entering your home.
